title: Rain Sensor + Servo Umbrella Deployment Module
design_question: How to automatically deploy/retract an umbrella bag in response to detected rainfall?
selected_approach: YL-83 resistive rain sensor module (LM393 comparator, digital out) detects rain. Arduino Uno debounces signal (200 ms), triggers SG90 servo (180° rotation) to pull deployment cord. Umbrella bag stored in housing; cord pull extends umbrella over load. Rain stop: sensor dry for 10 s delay, servo retracts. Optional: DHT11 humidity threshold cross-check to avoid false triggers from condensation.
rationale: Rain sensor + servo is <£3 total BOM, extremely simple, power-efficient (servo only active during transition). Software debounce prevents flutter triggering. Time-delay retract avoids repeated cycling in intermittent rain.
verification_notes: FACT: 'Rain Sensor — Resistive / Capacitive Detection Module' confirms R_wet < 10 kΩ trigger threshold and 50–200 ms response.
supporting_fact_titles: Rain Sensor — Resistive / Capacitive Detection Module
